title: Market Brief — Saturday, May 23, 2026
summary: Compute remains severely tight across all GPU classes. Util-avail stands at just 11% for A100, 14% for H100, 10% for B200, and 23% for H200, meaning nearly all rented capacity is spoken for. B200 spot dropped 5.9% day-over-day to $4.46/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, yet utilization still sits at 90.4%, signaling a pricing correction rather than demand destruction.
paragraphs: Compute remains severely tight across all GPU classes. Util-avail stands at just 11% for A100, 14% for H100, 10% for B200, and 23% for H200, meaning nearly all rented capacity is spoken for. B200 spot dropped 5.9% day-over-day to $4.46/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, yet utilization still sits at 90.4%, signaling a pricing correction rather than demand destruction.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), B200 inference runs $4.08/M tokens for Llama 70B, making it the only GPU where medium-tier pricing breaks even. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output on hardware costing roughly $0.56/M TCO-adjusted is selling at a loss of approximately $0.11 per million tokens. Grades: OpenAI Strong (premium moat via o3 Pro), Anthropic Neutral (Opus margins healthy but Haiku is thin), Google Neutral (Flash undercuts but sustains), DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at 10% util-avail and 54% of NeoCloud offerings still free is overvalued on spot at $4.46/hr given incoming supply and 105.9% annualized volatility. H200 at 23% util-avail with utilization climbing 5.3pp over 30 days is the undervalued capacity play: lock in $4.15/hr spot before tightening pushes it toward the $5.07 OCPI level.
bullets: 10% util-avail on B200 makes it the tightest GPU class despite a 5.9% price drop.
14% util-avail on H100 qualifies as severe shortage territory with 86.1% utilization.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 222x the cost of OpenAI's own gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M, the widest intra-provider spread tracked.
180.4% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is the highest of any GPU class, nearly 4x the A100.
$2/M output for DeepSeek R1 undercuts Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 by 83% at comparable reasoning capability.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token costs have fallen roughly 78% since GPT-4 launch.
